title: DNase I Treatment of Purified RNA in Solution for ISOLATE II Biofluids RNA Kit
author: Bioline
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.f5jbq4n
materials: Gently swirl tube to mix solution.

steps.title: In a micro centrifuge tube, mix together 2
steps.instruction: In a micro centrifuge tube, mix together 2.5 μL of the supplied DNaseI Solution, 10 μL of DNase I Reaction Buffer DRB, and up to 87.5 μL of eluted RNA. If using a lower starting volume of RNA solution, bring the volume up to 100 μL using RNase-free water.

steps.title: Gently spin down (approx
steps.instruction: Gently spin down (approx. 1s at 1000 x g) to collect solution at bottom of tube.

steps.title: Incubate at room temperature (18-25°C) for 10 min
steps.instruction: Incubate at room temperature (18-25°C) for 10 min. 00:10:00

steps.title: Repurify the RNA with a suitable RNA clean-up procedure, e
steps.instruction: Repurify the RNA with a suitable RNA clean-up procedure, e.g. using ethanol precipitation below.

steps.title: Add 1/10th volume of sodium acetate (3 M, pH 5
steps.instruction: Add 1/10th volume of sodium acetate (3 M, pH 5.2)

steps.title: Add between 2
steps.instruction: Add between 2.5 and 3 volumes of 96-100% ethanol to one volume of sample. Mix thoroughly.

steps.title: Precipitate for one hour at -20°C or overnight at -20°C
steps.instruction: Precipitate for one hour at -20°C or overnight at -20°C. 01:00:00 Note: Choose longer incubation times if the sample has a low RNA concentration. Shorter incubation times are sufficient for high RNA concentrations.

steps.title: Centrifuge at maximum speed for 10 min
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at maximum speed for 10 min. 00:10:00

steps.title: Wash the RNA pellet with ice-cold 70% ethanol
steps.instruction: Wash the RNA pellet with ice-cold 70% ethanol.

steps.title: Dry the RNA pellet and resuspend...
steps.instruction: Dry the RNA pellet and resuspend the RNA in RNase-free water.
